intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture. You should apply Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs. Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent consisting of a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID auth, Keychain, XPC channel, process management) and a Python 3.12 backend (consensus engine, build pipeline, GitHub integration, document store, multi-agent coordination, holistic review, TRD workflow, recovery/state management, CI pipeline, self-healing, and cross-run learning systems). The architecture is fully specified across 17 TRDs covering every subsystem from the macOS application shell (TRD-1) through self-healing software (TRD-17), with the five identified gaps (TRD-18 Crafted Connect, CommandRouter, EmailNotifier, Figma MCP, recover.py) to be resolved during PRD decomposition using best practices. This is a full-system build that will be decomposed into multiple PRDs, each producing 8-10 PRs per the documented constraints.

Clarifications:
  - Resolved during the PRD process. Use best practices to deploy.
  - Resolved during the PRD process. Use best practices to deploy.

Operator on 'Should the initial PRD decomposition prioritize building the':
both tracks proceed in parallel

Operator on 'For the v1 build, should we implement all three TRD-7 operat':
implement all three TRD-7 operator modes (FOUNDER, ENGINEER, CONSULTANT)

Operator on 'TRD-8 v3.0 introduces the Health Dashboard, self-healing rem':
included in the initial build scope. Resolved during the PRD process. Use best practices to deploy.

Operator on 'The Holistic Code Review (TRD-6) is described as an independ':
included in the initial build scope. Resolved during the PRD process. Use best practices to deploy.

prd_plan.title: macOS Application Shell — SwiftUI Chrome, Window Management & Process Lifecycle
Configurable Settings System & Environment Variable Management
Python Backend Skeleton, Plugin Architecture & CommandRouter
Document Store, Embedding Index & Cross-Run Memory (TRD-10/TRD-15)
State Management, Recovery & Crash Resilience (TRD-11 / recover.py)
End-to-End Integration Testing, Polish & Release Packaging

1.title: Project scaffold — directory structure and package init files
1.summary: Create the top-level directory layout for both Swift and Python sides: CraftedAgent/XPCBridge/, backend/crafted/ipc/, tests/xpc/, integration_tests/ipc/, and all __init__.py / .gitkeep files. No logic, no config — skeleton only.

2.title: Project configuration — pyproject.toml and Package.swift
2.summary: Add Python project configuration (pyproject.toml with Python 3.12 requirement, dev dependencies for pytest/pytest-asyncio) and Swift Package.swift for the XPCBridge module. No source code — build/dependency config only.

3.title: IPC protocol specification and architecture documents
3.summary: Add ARCHITECTURE.md describing the two-process model and XPC bridge role, IPC_PROTOCOL.md defining the JSON line wire format and message envelope schema (FR-2), HANDSHAKE_SEQUENCE.md for the nonce auth flow (FR-3), ERROR_ENVELOPE.md for error format (FR-5), and CONVENTIONS.md for naming and versioning rules. These are the canonical references for all implementation PRs.

4.title: Python message envelope types and serialization
4.summary: Define the Python-side message envelope dataclass (MessageEnvelope with type, id, session_id, timestamp, and arbitrary payload per FR-2.1). Includes UUID v4 generation for id field, Unix epoch millisecond timestamp generation, JSON serialization/deserialization with strict validation, and the ErrorEnvelope format (FR-5). Also includes protocol constants (version, message types). All types are pure data — no I/O.

5.title: Python line-delimited JSON framing codec
5.summary: Implement the line-delimited JSON read buffer (FR-1.3, FR-1.4): a StreamCodec class that accumulates raw bytes, splits on newline boundaries, yields complete JSON objects, and retains incomplete trailing data for the next read cycle. Handles fragmented reads and validates each line as valid JSON. Pure logic — no socket I/O.

6.title: Python configurable settings for XPC bridge
6.summary: Implement a Settings class for all configurable XPC bridge parameters: heartbeat interval/timeout, handshake timeout, reconnection backoff base/max, socket path template, and max message size. Loads from environment variables with documented defaults. Validates all values at startup. This is the single configuration surface for the XPC subsystem.

7.title: Python socket client transport primitive
7.summary: Implement the backend-side Unix domain socket client that reads the socket path from CRAFTED_XPC_SOCKET environment variable, connects to the Swift listener, and exposes line-oriented async read and write primitives using asyncio streams. No protocol logic — raw transport only.

8.title: Python nonce handshake and session authentication
8.summary: Implement the handshake protocol (FR-3): nonce validation logic, session_id assignment on successful auth, handshake timeout enforcement, protocol version negotiation, and rejection of messages before handshake completes. Includes nonce generation utility using secrets module. Pure protocol logic consuming MessageEnvelope types.

9.title: Python heartbeat protocol handler
9.summary: Implement the heartbeat/keepalive protocol (FR-4): HeartbeatService class that tracks last-received ping timestamps, sends pong responses, detects heartbeat timeout (missed heartbeats beyond configured threshold), and emits connection-lost events. Configurable interval and timeout via Settings. Uses envelope types but no direct socket I/O — accepts send/receive callbacks.

10.title: Python XPC server — connection lifecycle and message dispatch
10.summary: Implement the asyncio-based XPC transport server: wires socket client, StreamCodec for framing, handshake authentication, heartbeat monitor, and manages connection lifecycle (connecting, authenticated, disconnected states). Includes MessageDispatcher that maps message type strings to async handler callables, validates incoming envelopes, routes to registered handlers with request/response correlation via message id (FR-2.2), and returns error envelopes for unknown types. Includes graceful shutdown and socket cleanup.

11.title: Python reconnect and shutdown lifecycle policy
11.summary: Implement backend-side disconnect handling, graceful shutdown behavior, retry boundaries with exponential backoff, and restart-oriented state reset for the transport service. Integrates with server connection state transitions and heartbeat timeout events.

12.title: Python security hardening — permissions, entropy, and limits
12.summary: Security-focused hardening pass for Python side: validate nonce uses cryptographically secure random bytes (secrets module), enforce maximum message size limit to prevent memory exhaustion, add rate limiting on unauthenticated connection attempts, and ensure socket file cleanup on crash (atexit handler).

13.title: Python integration tests — handshake, messaging, heartbeat, reconnection
13.summary: End-to-end integration tests that start the Python XPC server and a lightweight Python socket client (simulating the Swift side) over an actual Unix domain socket. Tests cover: successful nonce handshake, session_id propagation, request/response correlation, heartbeat ping/pong cycle, heartbeat timeout detection, error envelope on unknown message type, fragmented message delivery, and graceful shutdown cleanup.

14.title: Swift XPCBridge protocol models and JSON coding
14.summary: Define Swift Codable types for message envelopes (MessageEnvelope with type, id, session_id, timestamp fields per FR-2.1), handshake messages, heartbeat messages, ErrorEnvelope (FR-5), protocol constants, and JSON encoder/decoder configured for the wire format. All pure value types — no I/O.

15.title: Swift line-delimited JSON framing codec
15.summary: Implement the Swift-side line-delimited JSON codec: a LineCodec class/struct that frames outgoing messages with newline terminators and parses incoming byte streams by splitting on newline boundaries with incomplete-data buffering (FR-1.3, FR-1.4). Mirror of the Python StreamCodec semantics.

16.title: Protocol interoperability fixture tests
16.summary: Cross-language fixture-based tests that verify Swift and Python implementations serialize and validate the same handshake, heartbeat, and error envelopes identically. Shared JSON fixture files serve as the single source of truth.

17.title: Swift Unix domain socket listener
17.summary: Implement the Swift-side Unix domain socket listener that binds at NSTemporaryDirectory()/crafted-{pid}.sock, sets socket file permissions to 0600 (owner-only), accepts a single backend connection, and manages socket file lifecycle including cleanup on deallocation.

18.title: Swift nonce handshake coordinator
18.summary: Implement the Swift-side handshake initiator (FR-3): generates a single-use nonce via cryptographically secure random bytes, passes it to the Python process via environment variable, sends the handshake message after connection, validates the handshake response, stores session_id, and enforces handshake timeout.

19.title: Swift XPCChannel actor — connection and message routing
19.summary: Build the high-level Swift XPCChannel actor that owns the listener, accepts the backend connection, wires the LineCodec for framing, performs handshake via HandshakeCoordinator, provides async send(MessageEnvelope) and receive() methods with request/response correlation via RequestRegistry, and manages connection state transitions (listening, connected, authenticated, disconnected).

20.title: Swift heartbeat monitor
20.summary: Implement Swift-side keepalive scheduling: sends periodic ping messages, monitors for pong responses, detects heartbeat timeout (missed heartbeats beyond configured threshold), and triggers connection-lost state transitions on the XPCChannel.

21.title: Swift connection lifecycle and reconnection policy
21.summary: Implement the Swift-side connection recovery logic: state machine for startup/authenticated/degraded/disconnected/cleanup states, exponential backoff reconnection attempts when the socket disconnects unexpectedly, Python process restart triggering after max reconnection failures, and state cleanup between reconnection cycles. Integrates with XPCChannel state transitions and HeartbeatMonitor timeout events.

22.title: Swift socket security hardening
22.summary: Security-focused hardening for the Swift side: validate socket permissions enforcement (0600), verify nonce entropy source is SecRandomCopyBytes, enforce maximum message size on receive path, and add audit logging for authentication events.

23.title: End-to-end cross-process integration tests
23.summary: Process-level integration tests that exercise the full Swift-Python transport path: socket startup, authenticated nonce handshake, request-response correlation, event delivery, heartbeat ping/pong, malformed message rejection, and disconnect recovery.

24.title: Transport edge case and observability tests
24.summary: Focused tests for lifecycle edge cases including fragmented multi-message input, stale nonce rejection, heartbeat loss cascading to disconnect, duplicate message ID handling, reconnect state reset correctness, and max message size enforcement.

25.title: Backend launch contract documentation
25.summary: Documents the required environment variables (CRAFTED_XPC_SOCKET, CRAFTED_XPC_NONCE), socket path injection contract, nonce provisioning expectations, version negotiation fields, and operational constraints for launching the Python backend from the Swift shell.

26.title: CI workflow for XPC bridge tests
26.summary: Add GitHub Actions CI workflow that runs the full Python test suite (pytest) and Swift tests (swift test) on macOS runners. Includes socket cleanup between test runs, timeout enforcement on integration tests, and test result reporting. Configured to trigger on changes to backend/crafted/ipc/ and CraftedAgent/XPCBridge/ paths. Also adds Makefile with test/lint/format targets.
